name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Concentrated Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Combustion Impact
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Happiny

abilities.name: Playhouse Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

flavorText: Usually it's a well-behaved Pokémon, but if you take its round rock away, it will cry and fuss and throw a big old tantrum.

name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Licks Go Crazy
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand, discard the top card of your opponent's deck, and discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A contest is under way to determine which one can stick its tongue out the farthest. The current record is…more than 82 feet.

name: Toucannon

attacks.name: Heat Beak
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Giganticannon
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Within its beak, its internal gas ignites, explosively launching seeds with enough power to pulverize boulders.

name: Escape Rope
rules: Each player switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. Your opponent switches first. (If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, that player doesn't switch Pokémon.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Mystery Plate β
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate β.

attacks.name: Stone Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, draw 3 cards. If your opponent has only 1 Prize, choose 2 Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon (1 if there is only 1). Your opponent shuffles those cards into his or her deck.

name: Tate & Liza
rules:
Choose 1:
• Shuffle your hand into your deck then draw 5 cards.
• Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done to the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal, whenever it damages a Pokémon, reduce that damage by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Lum Berry
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, it recovers from all of them, and discard this card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Cape of Toughness
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +50 HP, except Pokémon-GX.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Wash Water Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Water Energy.
Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to the Water Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

name: Nanu
rules: Choose a Basic Darkness Pokémon in your discard pile. Switch it with 1 of your Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Eevee-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ascension DNA
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have a Pokémon in your hand that evolves from Eevee, you may put that card onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Before evolving, heal all damage from this Pokémon. You can't use this Ability during your first turn or on the turn this Pokémon was put into play.

attacks.name: Joy Maker-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it's one of them. This way they won't attack it.

name: Rainbow Energy
rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

name: Nita
rules: You can play this card only if your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon.
Put an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon on top of their deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Baby-Doll Eyes
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon has excellent judgment. If it decides it can't defeat an opponent, it immediately turns tail and vamooses.

name: Adventure Bag
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Palkia-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spatial Control
attacks.text: Move any number of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pressure
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zero Vanish-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle all Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Tag Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your TAG TEAM Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Kangaskhan protects its child by keeping it in its pouch. It has zero forgiveness for those who harm its child and will beat them down.

name: Rillaboom V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Forest Feast
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Grass Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Multi Switch
rules: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Marnie
rules: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Familiar Bell
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as a Pokémon in your discard pile, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Mystery Plate γ
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate γ.

attacks.name: Retro Cave
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, shuffle your hand into your deck and then draw 6 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2 Prizes, choose 1 of your opponent's Evolved Pokémon. Your opponent puts the top card on that Evolved Pokémon on the bottom of his or her deck. (This counts as devolving that Pokémon.)

name: Orbeetle V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Magearna

attacks.name: Entertain
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prismatic Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of different types of Pokémon on your opponent's Bench.

flavorText: Magearna, with its metallic body, is an artificial Pokémon created 500 years ago by humans.

name: TV Reporter
rules: Draw 3 cards. Then, discard a card from your hand. If you have no cards in your deck, you can't play this card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Eevee-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ascension DNA
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have a Pokémon in your hand that evolves from Eevee, you may put that card onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Before evolving, heal all damage from this Pokémon. You can't use this Ability during your first turn or on the turn this Pokémon was put into play.

attacks.name: Joy Maker-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Sabrina's Suggestion
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Eldegoss V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Happy Match
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Float Up
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Herdier

attacks.name: Work Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The longer its black fur grows, the harder and more impervious it gets. Claws and fangs can't easily penetrate it.

name: Boost Energy
rules: Boost Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon. Discard Boost Energy at the end of the turn it was attached. Boost Energy provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy. The Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to can't retreat. When the Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to is no longer an Evolved Pokémon, discard Boost Energy.

name: Underground Expedition
rules: Look at the bottom 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Put the other cards back on the bottom of your deck in any order.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: M Gengar-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hollow Geist
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Mystery Plate δ
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Mystery Plate δ.

attacks.name: Healing Oasis
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 or more Prizes, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If your opponent has exactly 2 Prizes, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Celesteela-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Force Canceler
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, prevent all effects of your opponent's GX attacks, including damage, done to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Discovery-GX
attacks.text: Count your Prize cards and put them into your hand. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards. If you don't have that many cards in your deck, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Metal Saucer
rules: Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Metal Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Glimwood Tangle
rules: Once during each player's turn, after that player flips any coins for an attack, they may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again.
This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Aether Foundation Employee
rules: Put 3 Pokémon that have "Alolan" in their names from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zarude V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Jungle Rising
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

name: Rescue Stretcher
rules:
Choose 1:
• Put a Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
• Shuffle 3 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Celebi & Venusaur-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pollen Hazard
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Evergreen-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Grass Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), shuffle all cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Stoutland

abilities.name: Arf Arf Bark
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It pays no mind to the cold, thanks to its long warm coat. Stoutland in Alola look a little uncomfortable.

name: Meltan

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.text: Search your deck for Meltan and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

name: Shiftry-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Den of Iniquity-GX
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Crystal Energy
rules: Crystal Energy provides 1 Energy of all types (colors) of basic Energy cards attached to the Pokémon Crystal Energy is attached to. If there are no basic Energy cards attached to the Pokémon Crystal Energy is attached to, Crystal Energy provides Colorless Energy.

name: Garchomp-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Mystery Zone
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Once during each player's turn (before he or she attacks), if that player has an Evolution card in his or her hand, he or she may search his or her deck for a basic Energy card, show it to his or her opponent, and put it into his or her hand. Then that player chooses an Evolution card from his or her hand, shows it to his or her opponent, and puts it into his or her deck. That player shuffles his or her deck afterward.

name: Escape Board
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is Colorless less, and it can retreat even if it's Asleep or Paralyzed.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ninetales V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nine-Tailed Shapeshifter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Beast Bringer
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, and if your opponent's Active Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of the Ultra Beast this card is attached to, take 1 more Prize card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Every day, it tilts its head in the same rhythm. A long time ago, people raised these Pokémon to serve as clocks.

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Kabu
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Choice Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from the attacks of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Talonflame V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Flight
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Ordinary Rod
rules:
Choose 1 or both:
• Shuffle up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Magikarp & Wailord-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Towering Splash-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 7 extra Water Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Melmetal-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hard Coat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Force-GX
attacks.text: Attach any number of Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: With its powerful legs and sturdy claws, it can crack even the hard shells of Shellder and pluck out their insides.

name: Blaziken-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Explosive Kick
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blaze Out-GX
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Warp Energy
rules: Warp Energy provides 1 Colorless Energy. When you attach Warp Energy from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: M Garchomp-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

name: Missing Clover
rules:
You may play 4 Missing Clover cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, look at the top card of your deck.
• If you played 4 cards, take a Prize card. (This effect works one time for 4 cards.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Oracle
rules: You can play only 1 Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Choose 2 cards from your deck and shuffle the rest of your deck. Put the chosen cards on top of your deck in any order.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Field Runner
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

name: Galarian Darmanitan V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Freezing Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Frozen Slice
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Counter Gain
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless less.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: Blindside
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With eyes that can see in pitch-darkness, it never lets its prey escape. Some even call it "the emperor of dark nights."

name: Chip-Chip Ice Axe
rules: Look at the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck and choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck. Then, put the card you chose on top of their deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Old PC
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Pal Pad
rules: Shuffle up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Pikachu & Zekrom-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Blitz
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Tag Bolt-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 170 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Flare Starter
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it's your first turn, instead search for up to 3 Fire Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: High-Heat Blast
attacks.text: If you have at least 4 Fire Energy in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It's said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

name: Braviary

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aero Fall
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The more scars it has on its front, the more heroic it's considered to be. Those with many scars on their back are mocked by the flock.

name: Garchomp Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Garchomp-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Articuno-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Legendary Ascent
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move any number of Water Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cold Crush-GX
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from both Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Crystal Type
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water, Lightning, or Psychic basic Energy card from your hand to Kingdra, Kingdra's type (color) becomes the same as that Energy card type until the end of the turn.

attacks.name: Aquabomb
attacks.text: Kingdra does 10 damage to itself. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance when Kingdra damages itself with this attack.)

attacks.name: Dual Burn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each tails, discard 1 Energy card attached to Kingdra.

name: Star Piece
rules: Attach Star Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

At any time between turns, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is Benched and has 2 or more damage counters on it, search your deck for an Evolution card that Pokémon evolves into and put it on top of that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, discard Star Piece.

name: Milotic V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Peeking Red Card
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may have your opponent count the cards in their hand, shuffle those cards into their deck, then draw that many cards.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Piers
rules: Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Lazy Howl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, their turn ends.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

name: Custom Catcher
rules:
You may play 2 Custom Catcher cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
• If you played 2 cards, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (This effects works one time for 2 cards.)

You may play 2 Mixed Herbs cards at once.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.text: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

name: Dhelmise VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swinging Chain
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Max Anchor
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Max Anchor.

name: Eldegoss

attacks.name: Blessing of Fluff
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

name: Grookey

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Thwackey

abilities.name: Lay of the Land
abilities.text: If you have a Stadium in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

name: Rillaboom

abilities.name: Voltage Beat
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

name: Gossifleur
flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

name: Eldegoss

attacks.name: Enriching Seeds
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

name: Applin

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it's just an apple and nothing more.

name: Flapple

abilities.name: Apple Drop
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

name: Appletun

abilities.name: Delicious Aroma
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

name: Zarude

attacks.name: Suctioning Vines
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once the vines on Zarude's body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

name: Raboot

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Amazing Blaze
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: When Reshiram's tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world's weather.

name: Cinderace

abilities.name: Libero
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may attach up to 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to it.

attacks.name: Flare Striker
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

name: Sizzlipede
flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Field Runner
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Hundred Foot Flames
attacks.text: For each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

name: Cinderace VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Max Pyro Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Galarian Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

name: Horsea
flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Kyogre

attacks.name: Amazing Surge
attacks.text: This attack does 80 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

name: Galarian Mr. Rime

abilities.name: Shuffle Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards with the top card of their deck. (The cards stay face down.)

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It's highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

name: Buizel
flavorText: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

name: Floatzel
flavorText: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

name: Suicune

attacks.name: Aurora Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

name: Galarian Darumaka

attacks.name: Ice Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Ocean Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Pokémon you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Dynamite Steam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Galarian Darmanitan

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crushing Headbutt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Crushing Headbutt.

flavorText: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

name: Sobble
flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

name: Chewtle
flavorText: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

name: Drizzile

abilities.name: Shady Dealings
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

name: Drednaw

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Spit-Out Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.

name: Inteleon

abilities.name: Shady Dealings
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

name: Chewtle
flavorText: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

name: Snom

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

name: Drednaw

attacks.name: Vise Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

name: Frosmoth

abilities.name: Ice Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Water Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Under Pressure
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Luxio

abilities.name: Top Entry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn't full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Arrokuda
flavorText: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

name: Barraskewda

attacks.name: Spiral Jet
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy cards from your hand. If you don't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Luxio during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

name: Rotom

abilities.name: Roto Choice
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word "Rotom" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One boy's invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom's unique capabilities.

name: Snom

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Famished
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Famished
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Frosmoth

abilities.name: Ice Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Water Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Morpeko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Eiscue

abilities.name: Ice Face
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 60 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

name: Morpeko VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Discharge
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Indeedee V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Watch Over
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Dracovish

abilities.name: Primal Law
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.

flavorText: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

name: Arctovish

attacks.name: Hard Face
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cold Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Though it's able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

name: Rotom

abilities.name: Roto Choice
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word "Rotom" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One boy's invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom's unique capabilities.

name: Galarian Weezing

abilities.name: Neutralizing Gas
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Neutralizing Gas.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

name: Yamper

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

name: Boltund

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Fighting Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

name: Spinarak
flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

name: Crobat V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Asset
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can't use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Toxel
flavorText: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

name: Crobat VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stealth Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Risk Taker
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: When this Pokémon sounds as if it's strumming a guitar, it's actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Amazing Destruction
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Shocking Needles
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Nickit

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: Thievul

attacks.name: Call for Goons
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Dracozolt

attacks.name: Amping Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Amping Up attack does 90 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

name: Cufant
flavorText: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It's also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

name: Arctozolt

abilities.name: Biting Whirlpool
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

name: Ditto V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: V Transformation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may choose a Basic Pokémon V from your discard pile and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stick On
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

name: Galarian Ponyta

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

name: Ditto VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Transform
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Galarian Rapidash

abilities.name: Pastel Veil
abilities.text: Your Pokémon recover from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

name: Greedent V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stumbling Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Galarian Corsola

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

name: Cramorant V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Cramorant VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Jet
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

name: Galarian Cursola

abilities.name: Perish Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon's core spirit.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Helping Hand
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

name: Sinistea

attacks.name: Furtive Drop
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

name: Ball Guy
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Polteageist

abilities.name: Tea Break
abilities.text: You must discard a Pokémon that has the Mad Party attack from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

name: Boss's Orders
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Gym Trainer
rules: Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Hatenna

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

name: Hattrem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brutal Swing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rusted Shield
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
The Zamazenta V this card is attached to gets +70 HP.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Hatterene

abilities.name: Mind Hat
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Each player discards a card from their hand. (Your opponent discards first.)

attacks.name: Dripping Grudge
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

name: Rusted Sword
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
The attacks of the Zacian V this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Milcery

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

name: Team Yell Towel
rules: Heal 50 damage from both Active Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Alcremie

attacks.name: Decorate
attacks.text: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

name: Alcremie V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sugary Sprinkles
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sweet Splash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Replenish Time
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These intelligent Pokémon touch horns with each other to share information between them.

name: Dreepy

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

name: Drakloak

attacks.name: Summon
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Dreepy and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

name: Ball Guy
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dragapult

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Phantom Force
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When it isn't battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

name: Bird Keeper
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cara Liss
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Rare Fossil cards and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Galarian Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The Farfetch'd of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.

name: Galarian Sirfetch'd

attacks.name: Meteor Assault
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.

flavorText: Only Farfetch'd that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon's leek withers, it will retire from combat.

name: Gym Trainer
rules: Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Galarian Yamask

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask's dark power.

name: Piers
rules: Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Poké Kid
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Galarian Runerigus

attacks.name: Spreading Spite
attacks.text: For each damage counter on this Galarian Runerigus, put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mad Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

name: Rose
rules: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rolycoly
flavorText: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

name: Skyla
rules: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Carkol
flavorText: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

name: Alcremie VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Adornment
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: G-Max Whisk
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Coalossal

abilities.name: Tar Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Fire Energy card, a Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Silicobra

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

name: Sandaconda

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

name: Clobbopus

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

name: Grapploct

attacks.name: Octolock
attacks.text: Until this Grapploct leaves the Active Spot, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorless more, and the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. This effect can't be applied more than once.

attacks.name: Tough Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

name: Falinks

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Team Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has "Falinks" in its name.

flavorText: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass's orders are absolute.

name: Stonjourner

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

name: Galarian Weezing

abilities.name: Neutralizing Gas
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Neutralizing Gas.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

name: Galarian Zigzagoon

abilities.name: Headbutt Tantrum
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

name: Galarian Linoone

attacks.name: Night Slash
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

abilities.name: Untamed Shout
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Nickit

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: Thievul

attacks.name: Thief
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Impidimp
flavorText: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

name: Morgrem

attacks.name: False Surrender
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it's trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

name: Grimmsnarl

abilities.name: Dark Oath
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

name: Galarian Meowth

abilities.name: Evolution Roar
abilities.text: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Galarian Perrserker, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

name: Galarian Perrserker

abilities.name: Steely Spirit
abilities.text: Your Metal Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

name: Galarian Stunfisk

abilities.name: Snap Trap
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if it is Knocked Out), discard an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

name: Corviknight

attacks.name: Iron Wings
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

name: Cufant
flavorText: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It's also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

name: Copperajah

abilities.name: Antibacterial Skin
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Vengeful Stomp
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Metal Sharpener
attacks.text: Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Glance
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

name: Cinccino

abilities.name: Make Do
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

name: Ducklett
flavorText: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

name: Swanna

abilities.name: Sky Circus
abilities.text: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon's attack costs.

attacks.name: Feather Slice
attacks.text: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

name: Oranguru

abilities.name: Primate Wisdom
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.

flavorText: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

name: Skwovet
flavorText: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

name: Greedent

abilities.name: Greedy Tail
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.

name: Rookidee
flavorText: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

name: Corvisquire

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

name: Wooloo

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

name: Dubwool

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

name: Rillaboom V
