rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Forest Feast
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Grass Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Rillaboom VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Beating
attacks.text: You may discard up to 3 Grass Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Charizard VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Wildfire
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Centiskorch V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Radiating Heat
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Centiskorch VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Centiferno
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

name: Lapras V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Surf
attacks.text: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

name: Lapras VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Toxtricity V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Electric Riot
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Toxtricity VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Riot
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Indeedee V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Watch Over
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Falinks V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Iron Defense Formation
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have "Falinks" in their name take 20 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Grimmsnarl V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiky Knuckle
attacks.text: Put 2 Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

name: Grimmsnarl VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Drill
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each extra Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

name: Ditto V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: V Transformation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may choose a Basic Pokémon V from your discard pile and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stick On
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

name: Ditto VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Transform
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Dubwool V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Soft Wool
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Eternatus V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Accelerator
attacks.text: You may attach a Darkness Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamax Cannon
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Eternatus VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Eternal Zone
abilities.text: If all of your Pokémon in play are Darkness type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can't put non-Darkness Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)

attacks.name: Dread End
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Darkness Pokémon in play.

name: Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Zacian V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Intrepid Sword
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Metal Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Put the other cards into your hand. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Zamazenta V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dauntless Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VMAX.

attacks.name: Assault Tackle
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Orbeetle V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Galarian Mr. Rime

abilities.name: Shuffle Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards with the top card of their deck. (The cards stay face down.)

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It's highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

name: Polteageist

abilities.name: Tea Break
abilities.text: You must discard a Pokémon that has the Mad Party attack from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

name: Alakazam V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zen Spoon
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mind Ruler
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

name: Eldegoss V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Happy Match
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Float Up
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Electrify
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Cramorant V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Eevee VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Cuddle
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

name: Dragapult V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Assault
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Dragapult VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Phantom
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Crobat V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Asset
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can't use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Crobat VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stealth Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Venusaur V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Blastoise V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Torrential Cannon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Torrential Cannon.

name: Dedenne-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dedechange
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 6 cards. You can't use more than 1 Dedechange Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Tingly Return-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

name: Weepinbell

abilities.name: Dangerous Mucus
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned and Poisoned.

flavorText: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

name: Victreebel

attacks.name: Panic Vine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Once ingested into this Pokémon's body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

name: Cacnea
flavorText: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

name: Cacturne

attacks.name: Pull
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiny Punch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

name: Kricketune V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Exciting Stage
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand instead. You can't use more than 1 Exciting Stage Ability each turn.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Cherubi
flavorText: The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

name: Cherrim

abilities.name: Spring Bloom
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).

flavorText: The faint scent that emanates from its full blossom entices bug Pokémon to it.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Triple Whip
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Devour
attacks.text: For each of your Durant in play, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

name: Scatterbug

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

name: Spewpa

attacks.name: Grass Cocooning
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The beaks of birds can't begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

name: Vivillon

attacks.name: Vital Powder
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The patterns on this Pokémon's wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

name: Fomantis

attacks.name: Continuous Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It loves the sunshine. When it basks in the sunlight regularly, it grows up with vivid coloration.

name: Lurantis

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it slashes into its enemies, it looks like it's dancing. Its elegant appearance has led some to call it the most glamorous Grass Pokémon.

name: Tapu Bulu

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nature's Judgment
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Although it's called a guardian deity, it's violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

name: Blipbug
flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

name: Flapple V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sour Spit
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorless more.

name: Flapple VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Rolling
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Entei

attacks.name: Heat Dash
attacks.text: You may attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

name: Victini V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: V Bullet
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Flare Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Victini VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spreading Flames
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Max Victory
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Tepig
flavorText: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.

name: Pignite
flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

name: Emboar

abilities.name: Fighting Fury Stance
abilities.text: Your Single Strike Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Burning Licks
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Derisive Roasting
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.

name: Sizzlipede

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

name: Centiskorch

abilities.name: Overheater
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent flips a coin for their Burned Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup, it doesn't recover from that Special Condition even if the result is heads.

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Seadra
flavorText: It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

name: Kingdra

abilities.name: Deep Sea King
abilities.text: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move any amount of Water Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

name: Galarian Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Find It
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It'll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

name: Galarian Mr. Rime

attacks.name: Ball Juggling
attacks.text: Discard any number of Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name from your hand. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

name: Remoraid
flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

name: Octillery

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can't use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.

flavorText: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

name: Corphish

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.

name: Crawdaunt

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.

name: Empoleon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Emperor's Eyes
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Basic Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).

attacks.name: Swirling Slice
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Frillish

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

flavorText: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

name: Jellicent

attacks.name: Sediment Sink
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Water Energy card in your discard pile.

flavorText: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

name: Bruxish
flavorText: Its skin is thick enough to fend off Mareanie's spikes. With its robust teeth, Bruxish crunches up the spikes and eats them.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Haymaker
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Haymaker.

flavorText: While it's often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Lightning Slam
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Lightning Slam.

flavorText: A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.

name: Shinx
flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Jumping Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Electrostep
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scar Strikes
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Tapu Koko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiral Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Tapu Koko VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Shock
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, their Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Yamper

abilities.name: Ball Search
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Poké Ball card, a Great Ball card, or 1 of each from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

name: Boltund

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Defiant Spark
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for Lightning.

flavorText: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

name: Galarian Slowpoke

attacks.name: Tantailizing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Because Galarian Slowpoke eat the seeds of a plant that grows only in Galar, their tails have developed a spicy flavor.

name: Spoink
flavorText: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Self-Destruct
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Power Split
attacks.text: Attach Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like until your Pokémon and your opponent's Pokémon have the same total amount of Energy attached.

flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Pleasant Tone
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: There's enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can't be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.

name: Meowstic

abilities.name: Ear Moves
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It's when they're protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.

name: Mimikyu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dummy Doll
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Jealous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Necrozma V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Prismatic Ray
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Special Laser
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Dottler

attacks.name: Radar
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

name: Orbeetle

attacks.name: Evomancy
attacks.text: For each Energy attached to this Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon, except Orbeetle, and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Steamin' Mad Strike
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

name: Cubone
flavorText: It wears its mother's skull on its head, so no one knows what its bare face looks like. However, it's clear that it's always crying.

name: Marowak

abilities.name: Battle Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They thump their bones rhythmically to communicate among themselves. There are nearly 50 different rhythmic patterns.

name: Gligar
flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

name: Timburr
flavorText: Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.

name: Gurdurr
flavorText: Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there's skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.

name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Hammer Pressure
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.

name: Mienfoo

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Spinning Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When Mienshao lets out a bizarre wail, you're in danger. A flurry of kicks and chops too fast to see is about to be unleashed!

name: Rolycoly

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.

name: Carkol

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Coalossal

attacks.name: Coal Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: While it's engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

name: Silicobra
flavorText: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

name: Sandaconda

attacks.name: Big Sand Cannon
attacks.text: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Fighting Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

name: Falinks

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Squad
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

name: Stonjourner

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

flavorText: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.

name: Single Strike Urshifu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Laser Focus
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fighting Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

name: Single Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max One Blow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Rapid Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: G-Max Rapid Flow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Their skin is so thin that they'll be burned if sunlight hits them. When it gets cold out, they gather together to warm one another's bodies.

name: Golbat

abilities.name: Discreet Draw
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They can suck down over 10 ounces of blood in one go. They have been known to drink so much blood that they can no longer fly.

name: Crobat

abilities.name: Drastic Draw
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

flavorText: It feeds on the blood of living people and Pokémon. If it can't drink any blood for even a short while, it becomes weak and unable to fly.

name: Galarian Slowbro

attacks.name: Splattering Poison
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Unhinged Hammer
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: If this Pokémon squeezes the tongue of the Shellder biting it, the Shellder will launch a toxic liquid from the tip of its shell.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow's presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

name: Honchkrow

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Asleep.

attacks.name: Voltage Dive
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

name: Houndour
flavorText: They make repeated eerie howls before dawn to call attention to their pack.

name: Houndoom

abilities.name: Single Strike Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

name: Tyranitar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cragalanche
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Single Strike Crush
attacks.text: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Gather Food
attacks.text: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hangry Tackle
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Steel Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Powerful Vise
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Piercing Strike
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

name: Bronzor
flavorText: Polishing Bronzor to a shine makes its surface reflect the truth, according to common lore. Be that as it may, Bronzor hates being polished.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Metal Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Metal Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slash attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Steel Slice
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Metal Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.

name: Honedge

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people's lives away.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

attacks.name: Full Metal Blade
attacks.text: Discard 2 Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gigaton Bash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

name: Corviknight V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Sky Hurricane
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sky Hurricane.

name: Corviknight VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Lustrous Body
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: G-Max Hurricane
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use G-Max Hurricane.

name: Spearow
flavorText: Due to its short wings, it can't fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

name: Fearow
flavorText: Carrying food through Fearow's territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

name: Lickitung
flavorText: It licks filth clean with its tongue. Whatever it licks always stinks afterward, so whether it's really clean is…questionable.

name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Selickt
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses to discard the top 3 cards of their deck or to discard 3 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Pitch
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its tongue much more skillfully than its hands or its feet. It can deftly pick up a single small bean with its tongue.

name: Glameow
flavorText: When it's happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

name: Purugly

attacks.name: Caturday
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

name: Stoutland V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Dip Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Bouffalant

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent's Grass Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Head Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

name: Drampa

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hand Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

name: Bruno
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 7 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Camping Gear
rules: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Cheryl
rules: Heal all damage from each of your Evolution Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy from the Pokémon that were healed in this way.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Energy Recycler
rules: Shuffle up to 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Escape Rope
rules: Each player switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. Your opponent switches first. (If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, they don't switch Pokémon.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Exp. Share
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Fan of Waves
rules: Put a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon on the bottom of their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Korrina's Focus
rules: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Level Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Phoebe
rules: During this turn, damage from your Pokémon VMAX's attacks isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rapid Strike Scroll of Swirls
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
The Rapid Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Matchless Maelstrom
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rapid Strike Style Mustard
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Put a Rapid Strike Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
The Single Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Furious Anger
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Single Strike Style Mustard
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Search your deck for a Single Strike Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Sordward & Shielbert
rules: Choose a Trainer card from your discard pile. Then, ask your opponent if you may put it into your hand. If yes, put that card into your hand. If no, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Tool Jammer
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, Pokémon Tools attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon have no effect, except for Tool Jammer.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Tower of Darkness
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may draw 2 cards. In order to use this effect, that player must discard a Single Strike card from their hand.
This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Tower of Waters
rules: The Retreat Cost of each Rapid Strike Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is ColorlessColorless less.
This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Urn of Vitality
rules: Shuffle up to 2 Single Strike Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rapid Strike Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to a Rapid Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Rapid Strike Pokémon, discard this card.
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of Water Energy and Fighting Energy.

name: Single Strike Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to a Single Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Single Strike Pokémon, discard this card.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Fighting and Darkness Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time, and the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Cherrim

abilities.name: Spring Bloom
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).

flavorText: The faint scent that emanates from its full blossom entices bug Pokémon to it.

name: Octillery

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can't use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.

flavorText: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

name: Houndoom

abilities.name: Single Strike Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

abilities.name: Metal Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Metal Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

name: Arrokuda

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Arrokuda and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: First Step
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Persist Sting
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Jet Spear
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

name: Ledian
