flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

name: Inteleon

abilities.name: Quick Shooting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Crescent Glow
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it's your first turn, instead search for up to 3 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Photon Laser
attacks.text: If you have at least 5 Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Passimian

abilities.name: Throwing Coach
abilities.text: Your Rapid Strike Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Benched Pokémon V and Benched Pokémon-GX (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Throwing Coach Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Famished
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Strike Back
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Single Strike Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Celebi VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Curative Forest
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Plant
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Blaziken VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Max Blaze
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Rapid Strike Pokémon and attach an Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.

name: Blaziken VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Max Blaze
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Rapid Strike Pokémon and attach an Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.

name: Ice Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ride of the High King
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Lance
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Ice Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ride of the High King
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Lance
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Underworld Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Max Geist
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Underworld Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Max Geist
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Sandaconda VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sand Pulse
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: G-Max Cyclone
attacks.text: Move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Galarian Slowking VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Toxify
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 12 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

name: Metagross VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zap Traction
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Max Rush
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Max Rush attack does 150 more damage.

name: Tornadus VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Wind Spirit
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

name: Agatha
rules: Move up to 3 damage counters from your Active Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Avery
rules: Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, your opponent discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 3.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Brawly
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Rapid Strike Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Caitlin
rules: Put any number of cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck in any order. Then, draw that many cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Doctor
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Flannery
rules: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, and discard a Stadium in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Karen's Conviction
rules: During this turn, your Single Strike Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Klara
rules:
Choose 1 or both:
• Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
• Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Melony
rules: Attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Peonia
rules: Put up to 3 Prize cards into your hand. Then, for each Prize card you put into your hand in this way, put a card from your hand face down as a Prize card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Peony
rules: Discard your hand and search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Siebold
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon and heal 60 damage from each of them.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Electrode

abilities.name: Buzzap Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Lightning Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Metal Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Metal Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Gormandize
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Echoing Horn
rules: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Fan of Waves
rules: Put a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon on the bottom of their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Fog Crystal
rules: Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card or a Basic Psychic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rugged Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put an Energy attached to the Attacking Pokémon into your opponent's hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Urn of Vitality
rules: Shuffle up to 2 Single Strike Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Welcoming Lantern
rules: Put a Single Strike Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Hoppip

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

name: Jumpluff

abilities.name: Fluffy Barrage
abilities.text: This Pokémon may attack twice each turn. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Rally Back
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

name: Pinsir

abilities.name: Vise Coach
abilities.text: Damage from your Single Strike Pokémon's attacks isn't affected by your opponent's Active Pokémon's Resistance.

flavorText: Although it's tough, it can't handle cold well. When night falls, it buries itself in leafage and sleeps.

name: Skiploom

abilities.name: Solar Evolution
abilities.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

name: Leafeon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Max Leaf
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Dizzying Flower
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It's well liked by other Pokémon because of its beauty. The flower on its head needs constant care, or it will soon wither and rot.

name: Petilil
flavorText: They prefer clean water and soil. When the environment they live in turns bad, the whole bunch will up and move to a new area.

name: Crustle

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

name: Trevenant V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

name: Leafeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Greening Cells
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

name: Trevenant VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Missing in the Forest
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Supporter card in your opponent's discard pile.

name: Sharpedo

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jet Bite
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It's very sensitive to the scent of blood.

name: Glaceon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Crystal Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VMAX, except any Glaceon VMAX.

attacks.name: Max Icicle
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Wishiwashi

abilities.name: Group Power
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has 3 or more Water Energy attached, it gets +150 HP.

attacks.name: Schooling Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

name: Bergmite
flavorText: This Pokémon lives in areas of frigid cold. It secures itself to the back of an Avalugg by freezing its feet in place.

name: Carvanha
flavorText: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Element Chain
attacks.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Dracozolt VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Dracozolt V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Primeval Beak
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Energy cards can't be attached from your opponent's hand to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mountain Swing
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Vaporeon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Pod
attacks.text: Put a Water Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Torrent
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Gyarados V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Get Angry
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Eiscue

attacks.name: Blockface
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Enthusiastic Dance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may use this Ability. During this turn, your Basic Pokémon's attacks do 100 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.

name: Tympole
flavorText: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can't hear its cries of warning.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A professional swimmer, it can continue swimming for two days straight by waving its long tail skillfully.

name: Suicune V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fleet-Footed
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Blizzard Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Pikachu meet, they'll touch their tails together and exchange electricity through them as a form of greeting.

name: Glaceon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Awakening
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Heavy Snow
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

name: Psyduck
flavorText: It has been found that its brain cells are 10 times more active when Psyduck is experiencing a headache.

name: Eldegoss

abilities.name: Cotton Lift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

name: Tentacool
flavorText: Its body is 99% water. The remaining 1% contains the organ that makes its poison.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Poisonous Prison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It communicates with others of its kind by lighting up the red orbs on its head. When the orbs are blinking, it's a warning sign.

name: Lotad

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its leaf grew too large for it to live on land. That is how it began to live floating in the water.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Big Sparking
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electricity from the atmosphere.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Blinding Beam
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

name: Entei

attacks.name: Angry Fang
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Single Strike Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Synchrodraw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.

name: Litleo
flavorText: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

name: Pyroar

attacks.name: Rip Claw
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn't use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

name: Gyarados VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

name: Applin
flavorText: As soon as it's born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

name: Milotic

abilities.name: Dew Guard
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to you or your hand.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's said that a glimpse of a Milotic and its beauty will calm any hostile emotions you're feeling.

name: Avalugg

attacks.name: Frost Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: As Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon's body returns to a pristine state overnight.

name: Flareon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Detonate
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 100 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

name: Jolteon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Thunder Rumble
attacks.text: This attack also does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Ampharos

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electron Crush
attacks.text: You may discard 3 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That's why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

name: Gossifleur

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

name: Volcarona V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Surging Flames
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Flaaffy

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Victory Dive
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Lombre
flavorText: It lives at the water's edge where it is sunny. It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and becomes active at night.

name: Arctovish V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ancient Freeze
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Galarian Articuno

abilities.name: Cruel Charge
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Psychic Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psylaser
attacks.text: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Known as Articuno, this Pokémon fires beams that can immobilize opponents as if they had been frozen solid.

name: Hypno

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 90 more damage. Then, that Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

flavorText: There are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can't sleep at night in hospitals.

name: Regieleki

attacks.name: Teraspark
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar's electricity.

name: Drowzee
flavorText: It puts its prey to sleep and devours their dreams. It seems that bad dreams taste sour, so Drowzee doesn't particularly like eating them.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Mirror Pain
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon equal to the number of damage counters on 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

name: Espeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zen Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Espeon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solar Revelation
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached.(Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Max Mindstorm
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Synchro Woofer
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon's nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Go and Collect
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It feeds on gemstone crystals. In darkness, its eyes sparkle with the glitter of jewels.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

name: Golurk V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rewind Beam
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

name: Florges

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Connection
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move an Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its life can span several hundred years. It's said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

name: Flabébé

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it's collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

name: Floette

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

name: Sylveon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dream Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

name: Sylveon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Precious Touch
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Harmony
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

name: Pumpkaboo

abilities.name: Pumpkin Pit
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

name: Gourgeist

attacks.name: Pandemonium
attacks.text: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Psychic Pokémon you find there. Then, shuffle those Pokémon back into your deck and discard the other cards.

flavorText: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

name: Cutiefly
flavorText: An opponent's aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent's next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Bullet Straight Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

name: Ribombee

attacks.name: Tricky Steps
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.

name: Marshadow

attacks.name: Rapid Hunt
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Rapid Strike cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Shadow Flicker
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 1 more Prize card.

flavorText: It sinks into the shadows of people and Pokémon, where it can understand their feelings and copy their capabilities.

name: Medicham V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Yoga Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) If 1 of your Pokémon used Yoga Loop during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Hippopotas
flavorText: This Pokémon is active during the day and passes the cold desert nights burrowed snugly into the sand.

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Sand Press
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When roused to violence by its rage, it spews out the quantities of sand it has swallowed and whips up a sandstorm.

name: Galarian Zapdos

abilities.name: Strong Legs Charge
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Fighting Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zapper Kick
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One kick from its powerful legs will pulverize a dump truck. Supposedly, this Pokémon runs through the mountains at over 180 mph.

name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Pressure Shot
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

flavorText: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

name: Roggenrola
flavorText: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

name: Boldore

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It relies on sound in order to monitor what's in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it's facing.

name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Shaky Wave
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

name: Palpitoad
flavorText: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

name: Lycanroc V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Umbreon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Moonlight Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Lycanroc VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hunting Claw
attacks.text: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has 60 HP or less remaining.

attacks.name: Max Edge
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf's grass flute fills listeners with dread.

name: Umbreon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Signal
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

name: Galarian Moltres

abilities.name: Malevolent Charge
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Darkness Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fiery Wrath
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

flavorText: The sinister aura that blazes like molten fire around this Pokémon is what inspired the name Moltres.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Hard Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It protects itself with its durable skin. It's thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Shiftadieu
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any damage counters on it, put it and all attached cards into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Nipping Cyclone
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

name: Zoroark

abilities.name: Phantom Transformation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may choose a Stage 1 Pokémon, except any Zoroark, from your discard pile. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place.

flavorText: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.

name: Zorua
flavorText: If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

name: Garbodor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trash Stench
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Garbodor VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rubbish Collecting
abilities.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.

attacks.name: G-Max Malodor
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Tempting Tune
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Nickit
flavorText: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

name: Thievul

abilities.name: Fumbling Hands
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have each player shuffle their hand and put it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws 4 cards.

flavorText: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Hard Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Shelgon lives deep within caves. It stays shut up in its hard shell, dreaming of the day it will be able to fly.

name: Bagon
flavorText: Some theories suggest that its behavior of forcefully bashing its head into things stimulates cells that affect its evolution.

name: Salamence

abilities.name: Intimidating Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fierce Dragon
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its fervent wishes, the cells in its body finally mutated, and at last it has its heart's desire—wings.

name: Rayquaza VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Azure Pulse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Max Burst
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of basic Fire Energy or any amount of basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Chrono Wind
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Because it can't see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.

name: Rayquaza V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Burst
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 basic Fire Energy or up to 2 basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Extreme Freeze
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

name: Hydreigon

attacks.name: Dragon Counter
attacks.text: This attack does 100 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took during their last turn.

flavorText: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won't slow until their target goes down.

name: Noivern V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Boomburst
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Synchro Loud
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Zweilous
flavorText: Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.

name: Drampa

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Judgment Surge
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

name: Flapple

attacks.name: Acidic Mucus
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

name: Appletun

attacks.name: Thick Mucus
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

name: Duraludon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Breaking Swipe
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Regidrago

attacks.name: Dragon Energy
attacks.text: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.

name: Duraludon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Skyscraper
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have Special Energy attached.

attacks.name: G-Max Pulverization
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Live Painting
attacks.text: Reveal any number of basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

name: Teddiursa
flavorText: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Smack 'n' Slack
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it eats just three leaves in a day, it is satisfied. Other than that, it sleeps for 20 hours a day.

name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: Its stress level rises if it cannot keep moving constantly. Too much stress makes it feel sick.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Vee-Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Ursaring
flavorText: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

name: Slaking

abilities.name: Act Freely
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is the world's most slothful Pokémon. However, it can exert horrifying power by releasing pent-up energy all at once.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Tailwind Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 3 more cards.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.

name: Herdier

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has been living with people for so long that portrayals of it can be found on the walls of caves from long, long ago.

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is popular with beginners because it's intelligent, obedient to its Trainer's commands, and easy to raise.

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A combative Pokémon, it's ready to pick a fight with anyone. It has talons that can crush hard berries.

name: Braviary

attacks.name: Valiant Talons
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Because this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it's Corviknight that's taken the role of transportation in Galar.

name: Fletchinder

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

name: Stoutland

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Its fur is long and thick. A long time ago in cold regions, every household kept a Stoutland.

name: Aroma Lady
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you do, your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Nitro Dive
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

name: Crystal Cave
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may heal 30 damage from each of their Metal Pokémon and Dragon Pokémon.

name: Digging Gloves
rules: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Fighting Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Boost Shake
rules: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn. Your turn ends.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Copycat
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Elemental Badge
rules: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has "Vaporeon," "Jolteon," or "Flareon" in its name, its attacks cost Colorless less.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Full Face Guard
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has no Abilities, it takes 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Dream Ball
rules: You can play this card only if you took it as a face-down Prize card, before you put it into your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Gordie
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Lucky Ice Pop
rules: Heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, flip a coin. If heads, put this Lucky Ice Pop into your hand instead of the discard pile.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Moon & Sun Badge
rules: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has "Espeon" or "Umbreon" in its name, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to that Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Rubber Gloves
rules: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Lightning Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Rescue Carrier
rules: Put up to 2 Pokémon, each with 90 HP or less, from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Raihan
rules: You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Ribbon Badge
rules: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has "Sylveon" in its name and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Rapid Strike Scroll of the Flying Dragon
rules: The Rapid Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Meteor
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Shopping Center
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may put a Pokémon Tool attached to 1 of their Pokémon into their hand.

name: Single Strike Scroll of the Fanged Dragon
rules: The Single Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Superstrong Slash
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Spirit Mask
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), your opponent discards a card from their hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Snow Leaf Badge
rules: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has "Leafeon" or "Glaceon" in its name, it has no Retreat Cost and no Weakness.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Zinnia's Resolve
rules: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Switching Cups
rules: Switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Stormy Mountains

flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

name: Surfing Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Cosmoem

attacks.name: Star Protection
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: As it absorbs light, Cosmoem continues to grow. Its golden shell is surprisingly solid.

name: Surfing Pikachu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Surfer
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Lunala

attacks.name: Lunar Pain
attacks.text: Double the number of damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Shot
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It sometimes summons unknown powers and life-forms here to this world from holes that lead to other worlds.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.

attacks.name: White Thunder
attacks.text: If Reshiram is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Magma Volcano
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

name: Professor's Research (Professor Oak)
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Solgaleo

abilities.name: Rush In
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Geyser
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In writings from the distant past, it's called by the name "the beast that devours the sun."

name: Cosmog

attacks.name: Star Protection
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: Whether or not it's a Pokémon from this world is a mystery. When it's in a jam, it warps away to a safe place to hide.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

name: Flying Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Mysterious Tail
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal an Item card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Temporal Backflow
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Aero Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Deep Crush
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Professor's Research (Professor Oak)
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Zacian V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Roar of the Sword
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Storm Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Zamazenta V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Growl of the Shield
abilities.text: All of your Fighting Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VMAX (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Growl of the Shield Ability at a time.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Cry of Destruction
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Special Energy from your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Mysterious Tail
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal an Item card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all Energy attached to Charizard into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Rain Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 1 Water Energy card to 1 of your Water Pokémon. (This doesn't use up your 1 Energy card attachment for the turn.) This power can't be used if Blastoise is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

flavorText: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can't be used if Venusaur is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Here Comes Team Rocket!
rules: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.

abilities.name: Cosmic Power
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your hand and put them on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can't be used if Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, attach 1 of them to Rocket's Zapdos.

attacks.name: Electroburn
attacks.text: Rocket's Zapdos does damage to itself equal to 10 times the number of Lightning Energy cards attached to it.

name: Imposter Professor Oak
rules: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.

name: Dark Gyarados

abilities.name: Final Beam
abilities.text: When Dark Gyarados is Knocked Out by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this power does 20 damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Gyarados to the Pokémon that Knocked Out Dark Gyarados. Apply Weakness and Resistance. This power doesn't work if Dark Gyarados is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Normally found only in the deep ocean, it has recently been seen in shallow waters as well.

name: Shining Magikarp
rules: You can't have more than 1 Shining Magikarp in your deck.

attacks.name: Gold Scale
attacks.text: Your opponent may draw 2 cards. Either way, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Bond
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card named Gyarados, Dark Gyarados, or Shining Gyarados. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions, but never more than seven feet.

attacks.name: Birthday Surprise
attacks.text: If it's not your birthday, this attack does 30 damage. If it is your birthday, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

name: Cleffa
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything required in order to use that attack). If tails, your opponent's turn ends without an attack.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 7 cards.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

name: Rocket's Admin.
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player counts his or her Prize cards left and draws up to that many cards. (You draw your cards first.)

name: Umbreon ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

abilities.name: Dark Ray
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Umbreon Star from your hand onto your Bench, you may choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

name: Mew ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Versatile
abilities.text: Mew ex can use the attacks of all Pokémon in play as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Power Move
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to Mew ex. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, you may switch Mew ex with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Gardevoir ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Imprison
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Gardevoir ex is your Active Pokémon, you may put an Imprison marker on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Any Pokémon that has any Imprison markers on it can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies. This power can't be used if Gardevoir ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to Gardevoir ex to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Team Magma's Groudon
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: As long as the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) that has Team Magma in its name is 3 or less, Team Magma's Groudon can't attack.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Pulverize
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Luxray GL LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Luxray GL. Luxray GL LV. X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.

abilities.name: Bright Look
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Luxray GL LV.X from your hand onto your Active Luxray GL, you may switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

name: Garchomp C LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Garchomp C. Garchomp C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.

abilities.name: Healing Breath
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Garchomp C LV.X from your hand onto your Active Garchomp C, you may remove all damage counters from each of your Pokémon SP.

attacks.name: Dragon Rush
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Garchomp C. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Garchomp C can't use Dragon Rush during your next turn.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Xerneas-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Through
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: X Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use X Blast during your next turn.

name: Tapu Lele-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Wonder Tag
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Drive
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Tapu Cure-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Donphan by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: M Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Break
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Chespin
flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Inkay
flavorText: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

name: Froakie
flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: Honedge

attacks.name: Continuous Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

name: Snubbull
flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

name: Bunnelby
flavorText: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

name: Swirlix

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

name: Lotad
flavorText: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Sand Attack
