attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Meditite
flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

name: Marill
flavorText: The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is inept at turning because of its four short legs. It can only charge and run in one direction.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

name: Crabrawler
flavorText: It punches so much, its pincers often come off from overuse, but they grow back quickly. What little meat they contain is rich and delicious.

name: Grubbin
flavorText: They often gather near places frequented by electric Pokémon in order to avoid being attacked by bird Pokémon.

name: Popplio
flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was not originally found in Alola. Human actions caused a surge in their numbers, and they went feral. They're prideful and crafty.

name: Rowlet
flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Litten
flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Cosmog

attacks.name: Dust Gathering
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

name: Cutiefly

attacks.name: Fly Around
attacks.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

flavorText: It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it's able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

name: Alolan Diglett

attacks.name: Spelunk
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Its head sports an altered form of whiskers made of metal. When in communication with its comrades, its whiskers wobble to and fro.

name: Pikipek

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for the attacks it fires off from its mouth.

name: Yungoos
flavorText: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Known to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: As a result of headaches so fierce they cause it to cry, it sometimes uses psychokinesis without meaning to.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It's both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Pika Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its long tail often breaks off. It doesn't really feel any pain, though, and the tail grows back, so Slowpoke isn't particularly bothered.

name: Machop
flavorText: It loves working out. As it gazes at its muscles, which continue to swell day by day, it becomes more and more dedicated to its training.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it thinks of its deceased mother, it weeps loudly. Mandibuzz that hear its cries will attack it from the air.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: The eggs laid by Chansey are rich in nutrients and a favorite food of many Pokémon.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Searching Magnet
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Metal Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Dratini, a Dragonair, and a Dragonite, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Energy Evolution
abilities.text: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Code Check
attacks.text: Look at 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: Roughly 20 years ago, it was artificially created, utilizing the latest technology of the time.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

name: Tepig

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It blows fire through its nose. When it catches a cold, the fire becomes pitch-black smoke instead.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

name: Oshawott

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

name: Chespin
flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

name: Piplup
flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

name: Popplio
flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. The plant sprouts and grows with this Pokémon.

name: Grookey
flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Charmander
flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Litten
flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Snivy

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

name: Rowlet
flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Cyndaquil
flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Froakie
flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: Sobble

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Meal Time
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage plus Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

name: Hitmonchan
flavorText: While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

attacks.name: Focus
attacks.text: During your next turn, Rocket's Scizor's Agility attack's damage is doubled.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rocket's Scizor.

name: Professor Elm
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards. You can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.

abilities.name: Vine Pull
abilities.text: Once during your turn when Dark Ivysaur retreats, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Your opponent puts 3 markers onto his or her Pokémon (divided as he or she chooses). (More than 1 marker can be put on the same Pokémon.) Then, this attack does 10 damage to each Pokémon for each marker on it. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance. Remove the markers at the end of the turn.

attacks.name: Entrap
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Continuous Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Horrid Pollen
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. If both are tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Juxtapose
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch the number of damage counters on Rocket's Mewtwo with the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon (even if it would Knock Out either Pokémon). (It's okay if 1 of the Pokémon has no damage counters on it.)

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Crosscounter
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Hitmonchan is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, Rocket's Hitmonchan attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for double that amount of damage. (If Rocket's Hitmonchan takes 20 damage, it does 40 damage to that Pokémon.)

name: Reshiram & Charizard-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Flare Strike during your next turn.

attacks.name: Double Blaze-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fire Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Nonahex
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 9 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Feared for its wrath and the curses it spreads, this Pokémon will also, on a whim, cast spells that help people.

name: Sabrina & Brycen
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
When you play this card, you may discard 5 other cards from your hand. If you do, you may also search for up to 3 Pokémon of different types in this way.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Caterpie and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

name: Metapod

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can't withstand a harsh attack.

name: Butterfree

abilities.name: Tricolored Scales
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

flavorText: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

name: Shroomish
flavorText: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

name: Breloom V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

name: Pansage
flavorText: It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: Grass Munch
attacks.text: Discard a Grass Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Healing Circle
attacks.text: Heal all damage from each of your Benched Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Ditch and Shake
attacks.text: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They're jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

name: Shelmet
flavorText: It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.

name: Trevenant
flavorText: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon's feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

name: Phantump
flavorText: With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Ninja Tornado
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack can be used for Grass.

flavorText: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Bail Out
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements.

name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

name: Araquanid

attacks.name: Bubble Launch
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.

name: Dewpider
flavorText: Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.

name: Rillaboom V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Tsareena V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Queen's Orders
attacks.text: You may discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

name: Gossifleur
flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

name: Eldegoss

attacks.name: Sunny Wind
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

name: Rillaboom VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Drum Solo
attacks.text: This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Appletun V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sweet Impact
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Zarude

attacks.name: Wild Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Once the vines on Zarude's body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Byway of the Nine-Tailed Fox
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost of each of your Pokémon that has any Fire Energy attached is ColorlessColorless less.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

name: Vulpix
flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

name: Ninetales
flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

name: Vulpix
flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

name: Slugma
flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Warm Up
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has long been admired for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.

name: Magcargo

attacks.name: Body Splash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

name: Oricorio

abilities.name: Lesson in Zeal
abilities.text: All of your Fusion Strike Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Lesson in Zeal Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Glistening Droplets
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

name: Pansear

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Simisear

attacks.name: Fling Fire
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

name: Chandelure V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 40 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Chandelure VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Cursed Shimmer
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Max Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 70 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blaze Kick
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Fiery Cheering
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Exciting Flame
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack also does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 90 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: All-Out Shot
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Centiskorch
flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

name: Cinderace VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Fireball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Sizzlipede
flavorText: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

name: Sizzlipede

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

name: Shellder
flavorText: Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Multishot Star
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Water Energy from this Pokémon. Then, for each Energy card you discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It rotates its geometrically shaped body to swim through the water. It always seems to be sending out mysterious radio waves.

name: Cloyster

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Aqua Split
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.

name: Totodile
flavorText: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Splash Arch
attacks.text: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Soak in Water
attacks.text: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: No number of injuries can bother Staryu. Its amazing regenerative powers return it to its previous state in half a day!

name: Croconaw
flavorText: Once it bites down, it won't let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

name: Feraligatr

abilities.name: Rowdy
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you must flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.

name: Marill

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: Even after Marill swims in a cold sea, its water- repellent fur dries almost as soon as Marill leaves the water. That's why this Pokémon is never cold.

name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Dive and Rescue
attacks.text: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.

name: Qwilfish

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Marshtomp

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

name: Clamperl
flavorText: Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

name: Huntail

abilities.name: Single Strike Jammer
abilities.text: Your opponent's Single Strike Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more.

flavorText: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

name: Swampert

abilities.name: Muddy Maker
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card or a Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

name: Simipour

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Circus Soaking
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Supporter card you find there.

flavorText: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

name: Basculin

attacks.name: Swarm the Wound
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Known for their violence, these Pokémon have the most fights with schools of red-striped Basculin.

name: Galarian Darumaka

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The colder they get, the more energetic they are. They freeze their breath to make snowballs, using them as ammo for playful snowball fights.

name: Panpour

attacks.name: Pry
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

name: Gorebyss

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Canceler
abilities.text: Your opponent's Rapid Strike Pokémon in play have no Abilities.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.

name: Clauncher
flavorText: It moves around by jetting water from its right pincer. It has a poor sense of balance, so it's terrible at swimming straight.

name: Greninja V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dancing Shuriken
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

name: Galarian Darmanitan

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Daruma Headbutt
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for Water.

flavorText: Though it has a gentle disposition, it's also very strong. It will quickly freeze the snowball on its head before going for a headbutt.

name: Clawitzer

attacks.name: Snipe Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its right arm is packed with meat. When its pincer falls off, it's exported to be used as a cooking ingredient.

name: Inteleon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Crabominable V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trigger Avalanche
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Destroyer Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Pyukumuku

abilities.name: Pitch a Pyukumuku
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your hand, you may reveal it and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you do, draw a card. You can't use more than 1 Pitch a Pyukumuku Ability each turn.

flavorText: It's covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.

name: Inteleon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Double Gunner
abilities.text: You must discard a Water Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

attacks.name: G-Max Spiral
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

name: Arrokuda
flavorText: After it's eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That's when Cramorant swallows it up.

name: Chewtle
flavorText: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

name: Drednaw

attacks.name: Hard Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

name: Barraskewda
flavorText: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

name: Snom

attacks.name: Find Ice
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

name: Frosmoth

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Blizzard Loop
attacks.text: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Spark Duo
attacks.text: If 1 of your Minun used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Single Shot Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Sonic Boom
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

name: Minun

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

name: Shinx
flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Luxio
flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Luxray
flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Surprise Short
attacks.text: Discard all Pokémon Tools from all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: With a body made of plasma, it can inhabit all sorts of machines. It loves to surprise others.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

name: Helioptile
flavorText: The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Electro Blaster
attacks.text: Discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 200 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Upper Shock
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Eelektrik during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Charjabug
flavorText: Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Boltund VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Max Bolt.

name: Toxel
flavorText: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

name: Toxel

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Maximum Downer
abilities.text: If all your Pokémon in play are Fusion Strike Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon VMAX in play get -30 HP.

flavorText: It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Punk Shock
attacks.text: You may choose to make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed. If you do, this Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

flavorText: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

name: Mew V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Mix
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Leap
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Find Treasure
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it gets angry, it inhales with all its might, and its body gradually inflates. Sometimes they can grow 20 times larger!

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: The songs they sing are totally different depending on the region they live in. Some even sound like they're shouting!

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Targeted Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they're precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.

name: Mew VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross Fusion Strike
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Max Miracle
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Snubbull
flavorText: In contrast to its appearance, it's quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

name: Granbull

abilities.name: Dig Up
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Although it's popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it's totally incompetent as a watchdog.

name: Galarian Corsola
flavorText: Sudden climate change wiped out this ancient kind of Corsola. This Pokémon absorbs others' life-force through its branches.

name: Galarian Cursola

attacks.name: Force Regeneration
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon V from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench. If you do, put damage counters on that Pokémon until its remaining HP is 30.

flavorText: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You'll become stiff as stone if you touch it.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp Hold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

name: Munna
flavorText: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it's eating a good dream, and black when it's eating a nightmare.

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It drowses and dreams all the time. It's best to leave it be if it's just woken up, as it's a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Photon Boost
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Melodious Echo
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

name: Sandygast
flavorText: If you build sand mounds when you're playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.

name: Palossand

attacks.name: Oppressing Sandstorm
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They diligently serve people and Pokémon so they can gather feelings of gratitude. The females are particularly good at babysitting.

name: Dreepy

attacks.name: Infestation
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

name: Dragapult

attacks.name: Fusion Strike Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult's horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

name: Drakloak

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it'll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

name: Mankey
flavorText: If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Dig It Up
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: When its skin gets wrinkled from moisture, it heads for a volcano. It lies flat on a spot with a lot of geothermal heat and dries itself out.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Dig Uppercut
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Thanks to its thick claws, it's good at climbing trees. There are plenty of Sandslash that park themselves in trees and go right to sleep.

name: Geodude
flavorText: It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Gut Punch
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.

name: Golem

abilities.name: Desperate Blast
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 10 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It detonates its own body. The power from that explosion can propel it up steep mountain paths with amazing speed.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

name: Graveler
flavorText: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

name: Makuhita
flavorText: There's a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Venomous Hit
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

name: Baltoy
flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

name: Hariyama

abilities.name: Guts
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

flavorText: Hariyama that are big and fat aren't necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

name: Drilbur
flavorText: It's a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

name: Lucario V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthen Boom
attacks.text: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Raised Bad
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Darkness Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Wanting to make sure it's taken seriously, Pancham's always giving others a glare. But if it's not focusing, it ends up smiling.

name: Stufful
flavorText: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ancient Imprint
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 60.

flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

name: Bewear

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

name: Grapploct
flavorText: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

name: Falinks

attacks.name: Cliff Edge Formation
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

name: Clobbopus
flavorText: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn't alarmed when that happens—it knows they'll grow back. It's about as smart as a three-year-old.

name: Falinks
flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

name: Gengar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Slumber
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pain Explosion
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.

name: Gengar VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fear and Panic
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.

attacks.name: G-Max Swallow Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Galarian Linoone
flavorText: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

name: Tyranitar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Galarian Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

attacks.name: Silence
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Merciless Strike
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Carvanha
flavorText: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

name: Sharpedo
flavorText: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It's very sensitive to the scent of blood.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The elderly call it the disaster Pokémon and detest it, but interest in its power to predict disasters is on the rise.

name: Croagunk
flavorText: Once diluted, its poison becomes medicinal. This Pokémon came into popularity after a pharmaceutical company chose it as a mascot.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 4 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: It booms out a victory croak when its prey goes down in defeat. This Pokémon and Seismitoad are related species.

name: Darkrai
flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.

name: Zorua
flavorText: If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.

name: Mandibuzz

attacks.name: Bone Block
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Pokémon can't be played from your opponent's hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They adorn themselves with bones. There seem to be fashion trends among them, as different bones come into and fall out of popularity.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Knocking Hammer
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

name: Grimmsnarl

attacks.name: Rear Attack
attacks.text: If you have 2 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

name: Morgrem

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.

name: Galarian Meowth

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: These daring Pokémon have coins on their foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder coins garner more respect among Meowth.

name: Yveltal
flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Explosive Discontent
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

name: Impidimp

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It sneaks into people's homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Adversity Jaws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fire Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its body is draped in steel armor. It looks heavy, but it can fly at speeds of up to 185 miles an hour!
